ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-21-2009 IN DEPT.206, PLTF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A)&(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 8/10/09 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT.206. PLTF TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE DATES. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. ANY DISCOVERY MOTIONS WILL BE HEARD IN DEPT. 206. BY 5/22/09, PLTFS ARE TO PROVIDE AUTHORIZATIONS FOR THE MAYO CLINIC AND VA ATLANTA, AND ANY MEDICAL RECORDS AND DOCUMENTATION TO ESTABLISH THE RELATION BETWEEN PLTF AND HIS FATHER SO AS TO OBTAIN SOCIAL SECURITY RECORDS. THE DEPOSITION OF PLTF CAROLYN LEMASTER SHALL BE COMPLETED. ANY MOTION TO ENFORCE COMPLIANCE SHALL BE BROUGHT IN DEPT.206. PLTF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFT BY 5/22/09. IF PLTF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECL AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROP 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFT BY 6/12/09. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE E-SERVED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; REPORTER: SCOTT HUGHES

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2 (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY DESIGN AND MANUFACTURING DEFECT), GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1, 4 AND 5 (CIVIL BATTERY AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES) AND TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS TO ISSUE 3 (FALSE REPRESENTATION). BECAUSE OF THE COURT'S ORDER FILED JULY 21, 2009, DEFENDANT IS BARRED FROM SEEKING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2. DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT HAVE AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT COMMITTED CIVIL BATTERY AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PRESENTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THAT CLAIM. BECAUSE OF THE COURT'S ORDER FILED JULY 21, 2009, PLAINTIFFS ARE PRECLUDED FROM SEEKING PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFFS DISMISSED THEIR CLAIM FOR FALSE REPRESENTATION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. OREN NOAH OF BRAYTON & PURCELL, TEL. NO. 898-1555 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LISA ORBERG OF MCKENNA & LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP, TEL. NO. 267-4000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence and strict liability design and manufacturing defect), granted as to issues 1, 4 and 5 (civil battery and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). Because of the court's order filed July 21, 2009, defendant is barred from seeking summary adjudication as to issues 1 and 2. Defendant satisfied its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant committed civil battery and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on that claim. Because of the court's order filed July 21, 2009, plaintiffs are precluded from seeking punitive damages. Plaintiffs dismissed their claim for false representation. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Lavenna Ward, CSR # 7077
